A.missing: no two-cell union/fertilization; just something appears in body then lives inside
A.reason: Renders emergence of a new living thing inside body but omits the criterial fusion of two cells (ovum+spermatozoon).

B.missing: TOUCH of two cells present but no ovum/sperm as two distinct things uniting; scattered
B.reason: Has a small thing touching, inside body, then can live, but the two-gamete union is not clearly rendered.

C.missing: no becoming-alive, no body, no before/after emergence; just one thing touches another
C.reason: Bare 'something touches something' fits countless events; no pregnancy or new-life differentia.

D.missing: generic things-inside-things touching; no clear two gametes fusing to make one living thing
D.reason: Has TOUCH plus new thing there and living, but all-generic SOMETHING with no body/pregnancy anchor.

E.missing: two things exist, one does other, new thing comes to exist; no body, no fusion, no life-begins
E.reason: Skeleton of two things acting and a new thing appearing, but no fertilization/pregnancy criterial content.

F.missing: very-small someone appears inside body and lives; but no union of two cells/gametes
F.reason: Captures a new tiny living being coming to exist inside a body—pregnancy begins—but omits the fertilizing union of ovum and sperm.
